identity.mission: Create a 'Swiss Security Lab' aesthetic. Clinical, precise, and authoritative. Avoid generic SaaS 'friendly' vibes. This is a tool for professionals.
core_principles: Fusion: Swiss International Style x Cyberpunk Light Mode
Content: Data is the art. No decorations.
Negative Space: Massive padding, stark white voids.
Contrast: Black text on White background. No greys.

rules: Labels MUST be Uppercase + tracking-widest (0.2em) + text-xs
Code blocks MUST use JetBrains Mono

usage_rules: Background is ALWAYS Pure White (#FFFFFF) or extremely light grey (#F8F8F8).
Borders are visible and distinct (1px solid #E4E4E7).

features: Hero: Col-span-12 md:col-span-8 row-span-2. Stark white with massive typography.
Visual: Col-span-6 md:col-span-4. Abstract geometric security imagery.

rules: Create a 'Grid Border' look by setting container bg to grey and children bg to white with 1px gap.
Every widget is a rectangle. No rounded corners > sm.

variants.default: bg-primary text-white hover:bg-primary/90 shadow-none border border-transparent

cards.classes: border border-border bg-card text-card-foreground rounded-none shadow-none

data_display.tables: Dense, monospace font for data cells, uppercase headers.
data_display.charts: Recharts with custom tooltip. Use palette colors (Blue, Red, Black).

visual_enhancers.micro_interactions: Buttons click down (scale-95). Links underline expands from center.

hero_background.description: Use as a subtle texture or hero image with low opacity overlay.

feature_network.description: For 'Port Scanning' or network analysis features.

accessibility.contrast_check: Ensure all text is APCA compliant. Black on White is safe. Avoid light grey text.

must_do: Maintain 1px borders on everything to reinforce the grid

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. INSTALL FONTS: Add the Google Fonts import to public/index.html or CSS.
2. CONFIGURE TAILWIND: Update tailwind.config.js to use the 'Swiss' palette variables defined above. Force 'light' theme.
3. COMPONENT OVERRIDES: When using Shadcn, strip the radius and shadows. Make them flat and bordered.
4. LAYOUT: Build the Dashboard using a dense grid. Do not use floating cards. Use a 'bento' style where borders separate content.
5. MOCK DATA: Use realistic security data (IPs, Hashes, Vulnerabilities) in the UI.
6. FILE EXTENSIONS: Use .js and .jsx. The project is NOT TypeScript.
